description_long: This is a solid-body electric guitar with a distinctive V-shaped body, finished in a glossy red color with a white pickguard. The guitar features two humbucker pickups, three control knobs, and a three-way toggle switch, all mounted on the pickguard. The neck is dark, likely rosewood or ebony, with dot inlays and a set of six tuning pegs arranged in a 3+3 configuration on the headstock. The bridge is a fixed type, and the strings run from the bridge to the headstock, passing over two visible pickups. The overall design is reminiscent of the iconic Gibson Flying V, known for its sharp, angular lines and modern aesthetic. The hardware appears to be chrome or nickel-plated, adding a metallic accent to the instrument's bold appearance.
description: A red, V-shaped electric guitar with a white pickguard, two humbucker pickups, and chrome hardware. The neck features dot inlays and a 3+3 headstock with six tuning pegs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the V-shaped body, white pickguard, and chrome hardware, with the neck extending to the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical V body, control knobs, pickups, and the full length of the neck and headstock.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the body thickness, pickguard, and hardware layout, with the neck extending to the left.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the V-shaped body and the back of the neck, with tuning pegs visible at the headstock.
attribute_headstock_design: Angled 3+3 headstock with six tuning pegs, no visible logo, and a simple, contoured shape
